title: Speaking for a cause
setting: At a community meeting about fixing the neighborhood park, you're asked to make the case. The skill: persuasive, eloquent advocacy.

steps.speaker: Vecino
steps.es: ¿Y por qué deberíamos gastar en ese parque viejo?
steps.en: And why should we spend on that old park?

options.es: Porque no es solo un parque; es el corazón del barrio.
options.en: Because it's not just a park; it's the heart of the neighborhood.
options.feedback: Reframing from cost to meaning — that's persuasive rhetoric. Opens hearts, not just minds.
Too thin to move anyone. Advocacy needs a vision, not just a complaint.

options.es: Porque nuestros hijos merecen un lugar seguro.
options.en: Because our children deserve a safe place.
options.feedback: Appeals to shared values — children, safety. Emotionally resonant and effective.

steps.speaker: Vecino
steps.es: Pero no tenemos dinero para eso.
steps.en: But we don't have money for that.

options.es: Si nos organizamos, juntos podemos lograr más de lo que creemos.
options.en: If we organize, together we can achieve more than we think.
options.feedback: Conditional 'si nos organizamos' plus a hopeful vision — rallying language at its best.
Dismissing a real concern alienates people. Address it, don't belittle it.

options.es: Podemos pedir ayuda y aportar entre todos.
options.en: We can ask for help and all chip in.
options.feedback: Concrete and inclusive — turns an objection into a shared plan. Smart advocacy.

steps.speaker: Vecino
steps.es: Suena bonito, pero ¿de verdad cambiaría algo?
steps.en: Sounds nice, but would it really change anything?

options.es: Un barrio que cuida sus espacios se cuida a sí mismo.
options.en: A neighborhood that cares for its spaces cares for itself.
options.feedback: Aphoristic and quotable — the kind of line that sticks in people's minds. Eloquent.

options.es: Tal vez no, pero hay que intentar.
options.en: Maybe not, but we have to try.
options.feedback: Honest, but it concedes too much ground. Lead with conviction, not doubt.

options.es: Imaginen a los niños jugando ahí de nuevo.
options.en: Imagine the children playing there again.
options.feedback: Painting a vivid picture is powerful persuasion — you make them feel the future.

steps.speaker: Vecino
steps.es: Bueno... el chele habla con razón. ¡Cuenten conmigo!
steps.en: Well... the foreigner makes sense. Count me in!

options.es: ¡Eso! Juntos vamos a recuperar lo nuestro.
options.en: Yes! Together we'll reclaim what's ours.
options.feedback: Rallying and unifying — 'lo nuestro' makes it collective. A rousing close.

options.en: Thank you. I knew you'd understand.
options.feedback: Gracious and affirming — credits them for coming around. Warm leadership.
Condescending — it insults everyone who hesitated. You just won them over; don't lose them now.
